records.ety: [OE. contenen, conteinen, F. contenir, fr. L. continere, -tentum; con- + tenere to hold. See Tenable, and cf. Countenance.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Contained (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Containing.]

defs: To hold within fixed limits; to comprise; to include; to inclose; to hold.
quotes: Behold, heaven and the heaven of heavens can not contain thee; how much less this house!
When that this body did contain a spirit.
What thy stores contain bring forth.

defs: To have capacity for; to be able to hold; to hold; to be equivalent to; as, a bushel contains four pecks.
To put constraint upon; to restrain; to confine; to keep within bounds.
quotes: The king's person contains the unruly people from evil occasions.
Fear not, my lord: we can contain ourselves.

defs: To restrain desire; to live in continence or chastity.
quotes: But if they can not contain, let them marry.
